title: Complete Game Definition
description: Top-level schema that assembles all story components into a single validated game package. A pipeline can load one game file and have everything it needs to run a full session. The world, characters, and arc are validated against their individual schemas via $ref. This is the file the runtime engine consumes at game start.

meta.description: Top-level game metadata. Used for discovery, versioning, and display. None of these fields affect runtime behavior directly, but the pipeline reads duration_target_minutes to calibrate word budgets globally.

id.description: Globally unique game identifier. Must match story_id in arc and any cross-file references. Use kebab-case.

title.description: Display title shown in the game selection UI (e.g., 'The Last Session').

tagline.description: Optional one-line marketing copy shown under the title.

genre.description: Primary genre label (e.g., 'horror', 'mystery', 'sci-fi', 'drama'). Used for filtering and setting default narrator tone priors.

content_warnings.description: Optional list of content warning tags displayed before the player starts (e.g., ['psychological horror', 'themes of death', 'implied violence']).

duration_target_minutes.description: Intended total play duration in minutes. Must match world.duration_target_minutes. The pipeline uses this to validate that total beat word budgets are within range.

player_role.description: How the player is framed within the story — the role they inhabit (e.g., 'You are a therapist. You are Dr. Mercer. You are a detective'). Injected directly into the opening system prompt.

version.description: Optional semantic version string for this game file (e.g., '1.0.0').

language.description: BCP-47 language code for this game's content (e.g., 'en', 'en-US'). Defaults to 'en' if omitted.

world.description: The world definition for this game. Must conform to world-schema.json. The world.id should be consistent with meta.id, but they can differ if the world is reused across games.

characters.description: All characters that appear in this game. Must contain at least one character. The pipeline builds a character registry from this array keyed by character.id. At minimum, one character must have role='narrator' to provide the story voice.

arc.description: The story arc for this game. Must conform to arc-schema.json. arc.story_id must match meta.id.

prompts.description: The LLM prompt layer. Contains the base system prompt and optional per-phase overrides. The pipeline assembles the active system prompt by combining: system base + active character card + current phase override (if any) + current beat purpose + world physics rules.

system.description: The base system prompt injected at the start of every API call. Should define the narrator's fundamental role, voice, and constraints that apply across all phases. Character-specific and phase-specific directives are appended on top of this. Keep under 500 tokens — it is injected every call.

phase_overrides.description: Optional map of phase.id to an additional prompt string appended to the system prompt only when that phase is active. Use for phase-specific framing that does not belong in character_behavior (e.g., structural constraints, tonal shifts the narrator model needs explicit instruction for).

intent_classifier_prompt.description: Optional system prompt for the intent classification model (e.g., Mistral Small). If omitted, the pipeline uses a default intent classifier prompt with the world.player_can list injected.

fallback_response.description: Optional fallback narrator line if the LLM call fails or times out. Should be in-character and non-committal (e.g., '... I'm sorry. I lost my train of thought. What were you asking?').

sounds.description: The complete sound asset registry for this game. The pipeline reads this at init time to preload all required audio assets. cue_map covers one-shot reactive sounds; timeline covers deterministic timed sounds.

cue_map.description: Registry of all one-shot sound effects that can be triggered reactively during gameplay. Keys are sound ids (referenced in arc beat sound_cues). Values are cue definitions.

description.description: What this sound is and when it is used (e.g., 'Single sharp knock on wood. Used when Elara mentions the door').

asset_path.description: Relative path to the audio file from the game assets root.

tag.description: Optional tag the narrator can embed in its response text to trigger this cue (e.g., '[knock]'). The pipeline strips tags before TTS and fires the cue.

timeline.description: Deterministic timed sound events that fire at specific elapsed game times regardless of player actions. Used for atmospheric punctuation the designer wants to guarantee. The pipeline fires these in a parallel timer track.

time.description: Elapsed game time at which to fire this event in MM:SS format.

sound_id.description: Sound id from cue_map to play at this time.

condition.description: Optional state condition that must be true for this event to fire. Allows conditional timeline sounds without authoring complex beat logic.

description.description: Why this timed event exists — authoring note.

preload.description: Optional explicit list of asset paths to preload at game init. If omitted, the pipeline preloads all assets in cue_map and ambient_baseline.

evaluation.description: Post-game quality and analytics framework. The quality_checklist is used by the pipeline (and human reviewers) to verify a completed run met authorial intent. Metric definitions power the analytics dashboard.

quality_checklist.description: Ordered list of authorial intent statements a completed run should satisfy. Used for automated QA (LLM-as-judge), human playtest review, and regression testing. Each item is a plain-English criterion.

criterion.description: The quality statement to evaluate (e.g., 'The narrator never broke character', 'At least one choice beat was reached', 'The ending matched the player style score').

evaluation_method.description: Optional. How this criterion is checked: 'automatic' (pipeline checks game state), 'llm-judge' (send transcript to evaluator model), 'human' (manual review required).

priority.description: Optional severity if this criterion fails: 'blocking' (breaks the experience), 'important' (degrades quality), 'minor' (nice to fix).

metrics.description: Optional runtime metrics to collect and report after each session. Keys are metric names; values define how they are computed.

description.description: What this metric measures and why it matters.

source.description: Where the metric value comes from (e.g., 'game_state.elara.trust_level at end', 'count of choice beats reached', 'total elapsed seconds').

target_range.description: Optional min/max target range for this metric in a healthy run.

golden_path.description: Optional specification of the intended demo run — the sequence of choices that leads to the primary intended ending. Used for automated demo testing and pitch demos.

description.description: Human-readable description of the golden path (e.g., 'The compassionate playthrough that ends with Elara choosing to stay').

choice_sequence.description: Ordered list of {beat_id, option_id} pairs representing the demo choice sequence.

expected_ending_id.description: The ending_condition id that should fire after following this choice sequence.
